search_queries_used: site:asahq.org post dural puncture headache management statement diagnosis follow-up
site:rcoa.ac.uk post dural puncture headache follow-up
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Treatment selection, timing, technique, and consent were outside the documentation-only mapping.
Secondary reviews were unnecessary once the exact official ASA statement was opened.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Exact primarily for obstetric patients after recognised or suspected unintentional dural puncture; non-obstetric spinal-anesthesia headache remains partial.
setting_applicability: Direct for neuraxial follow-up, discharge, and post-discharge assessment; emergency neurological evaluation remains clinician-led.
UAE_applicability: International ASA statement requires UAE obstetric, anesthesia, neurology, imaging, consent, and follow-up pathways.
recency_verification: Official ASA statement approved 2021 was opened on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer ASA PDPH statement was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact ASA section supports documenting the puncture context, headache timing and features, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, and focused assessment without automatically diagnosing PDPH.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting the dural-puncture context, headache timing and features, associated symptoms, focused history and examination, and atypical or severe neurological features without automatically diagnosing PDPH.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The follow-up section supports recording follow-up and contact advice only when actually arranged or discussed by the clinician.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documented follow-up until symptom resolution, brief neurological assessment, and verbal or written contact advice as clinician-completed actions without generating treatment.

unresolved_source_gaps: Exact spinal or epidural procedure, puncture recognition, postural character, neurological findings, blood pressure and pregnancy context, differential diagnosis, imaging, consultation, treatment, response, and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
